questions.question: What is the difference between a class and an object?
questions.answer: A class defines the structure and behavior, whereas an object is a runtime instance of that class.

questions.question: What is a non-correlated subquery?
questions.answer: A non-correlated subquery executes independently of the outer query and returns a result used by the outer query.

questions.question: Which Python data types are immutable?
questions.answer: int, float, bool, str, tuple, frozenset, and bytes are immutable.

questions.question: What is the Data Link Layer?
questions.answer: The Data Link Layer provides node-to-node communication and error detection.

questions.question: What is an IPS?
questions.answer: An Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) monitors network traffic and actively blocks detected threats.

questions.question: What is an index in DBMS?
questions.answer: An index is a data structure that improves the speed of data retrieval operations.

questions.question: What is the CAP Theorem?
questions.answer: The CAP Theorem states that a distributed system can guarantee at most two of Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance simultaneously.

questions.question: What are the advantages of distributed systems?
questions.answer: Scalability, fault tolerance, high availability, resource sharing, and improved performance.

questions.question: What is a clustered index?
questions.answer: A clustered index determines the physical order of rows in a table.

questions.question: What is the Observer pattern?
questions.answer: The Observer pattern establishes a one-to-many dependency so that observers are notified when the subject changes state.

questions.question: What is a medium-term scheduler?
questions.answer: A medium-term scheduler temporarily removes processes from memory and later reintroduces them.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of BFS?
questions.answer: The time complexity of BFS is O(V + E), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges.

questions.question: What is the difference between a mutex and a semaphore?
questions.answer: A mutex has ownership and can only be unlocked by the thread that locked it, whereas a semaphore does not have ownership restrictions.

questions.question: What is an exclusive lock?
questions.answer: An exclusive lock allows a transaction to read and modify a data item while preventing access by others.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the except block?
questions.answer: The except block handles exceptions raised in the corresponding try block.

questions.question: What is the difference between an array and a linked list?
questions.answer: Arrays store elements in contiguous memory and allow constant-time indexing, whereas linked lists store elements in nodes connected by pointers and allow efficient insertions and deletions.
